courses.name: Kuika ile Web ve Mobil Uygulama Geliştirme
courses.purpose: Bu ders, öğrencilerin düşük kodlu bir platform olan Kuika’yı kullanarak web ve mobil uygulama geliştirme süreçlerini öğrenmelerini sağlar. Hızlı prototipleme, dağıtım ve yineleme becerilerini geliştirmeyi amaçlar.
courses.description: Bu ders, web ve mobil uygulamalar geliştirmek için kullanılan düşük kodlu bir platform olan Kuika’ya giriş sağlar. Öğrenciler, Kuika’nın görsel geliştirme ortamını kullanarak kullanıcı arayüzleri tasarlamayı, iş akışlarını tanımlamayı ve veri kaynaklarını entegre etmeyi öğrenirler. Ders, hızlı prototipleme, dağıtım ve yineleme süreçlerine vurgu yapar. Projeler kapsamında, girişimcilik ve kurumsal çözümlere uygun, minimum kodlama ile gerçek dünya uygulamaları geliştirilir.

courses.name: Special Topics in Computer Networks
courses.purpose: This course explores advanced and emerging topics in the field of computer networks and encourages students to examine current trends and research directions.
courses.description: The course covers advanced topics that arise in computer networks, including software-defined networking (SDN), network function virtualization (NFV), 5G technologies, IoT networks, and cybersecurity. It is research-oriented and encourages students to analyze protocols and develop experimental network applications or simulations.

courses.name: Robotics and Autonomous Robot Systems
courses.purpose: This course introduces the fundamental principles of robotics with a focus on autonomous behavior. It aims to equip students with the concepts and tools needed to design, model, and implement autonomous robotic systems.
courses.description: This course covers the basic principles of robotics, including kinematics, control systems, localization, path planning, and sensor integration. Students work with robotics platforms and simulation environments such as ROS and Gazebo to develop autonomous navigation and object manipulation systems. Application areas include unmanned aerial vehicles (drones), driverless vehicles, and industrial automation systems.

courses.name: Fundamentals of E-Commerce
courses.purpose: This course introduces the technologies and business strategies behind electronic commerce. It aims to help students understand how e-commerce systems are designed, secured, and operated in real-world settings.
courses.description: The course provides a comprehensive introduction to the technologies and business strategies behind electronic commerce. Topics include online transaction processing, digital marketing, e-payment systems, cybersecurity, and legal frameworks. Students examine case studies of successful e-commerce platforms and develop their own e-commerce solutions using tools such as Shopify, WooCommerce, or custom-built platforms. The course emphasizes rapid application development and producing solutions suitable for real-world scenarios.

courses.name: Wireless Mobile Networks
courses.purpose: This course aims to introduce the architecture, protocols, and technologies used in wireless and mobile communication networks. It also helps students understand how mobility, performance, security, and handoff mechanisms are managed in such networks.
courses.description: This course examines the architecture, protocols, and technologies of wireless and mobile communication networks. Topics include cellular networks (3G/4G/5G), Wi-Fi, Bluetooth, mobile IP, and handoff strategies. Students analyze network performance, security, and mobility management, and perform network simulation and configuration using tools such as NS-3 or Wireshark in laboratory work.

courses.name: Introduction to Control Systems
courses.purpose: This course introduces the fundamental principles of control systems used in engineering and automation. It aims to provide students with the conceptual foundation needed to analyze, model, and design basic control systems for practical applications.
courses.description: Topics include feedback control, transfer functions, stability analysis, PID controllers, and state-space representation. Students gain hands-on experience in modeling and simulating control systems using MATLAB/Simulink. Application areas include robotics, automotive systems, and industrial automation.

courses.name: Bioinformatics
courses.purpose: This interdisciplinary course aims to analyze biological data by combining biology, computer science, and statistics.
courses.description: Topics include DNA/RNA sequence analysis, genome annotation, protein structure prediction, and phylogenetics. Students use tools such as BLAST, Biopython, and R for data analysis. Application areas include personalized medicine, drug discovery, and evolutionary studies.

courses.name: Information Security
courses.purpose: This course aims to introduce the principles and practices used to secure information systems. It helps students understand how to identify vulnerabilities, apply security policies, and respond to security incidents.
courses.description: This course covers the principles and applications of information system security. Topics include cryptography, access control, network security, malware analysis, and risk management. Practical laboratory work includes penetration testing, secure coding, and the use of tools such as Wireshark, Metasploit, and Nessus.
learning_outcomes: Identify security vulnerabilities in information systems.
Apply access control and security policy mechanisms.
Analyze malware and network security threats.
Perform basic incident response and risk management activities.
Use common security tools for penetration testing and vulnerability assessment.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to information security and security principles
Hafta 2: Cryptography basics
Hafta 3: Access control models
Hafta 4: Network security fundamentals
Hafta 5: Malware analysis
Hafta 6: Risk management
Hafta 7: Vulnerability identification
Hafta 8: Security policies and governance
Hafta 9: Incident response
Hafta 10: Secure coding practices
Hafta 11: Penetration testing laboratory
Hafta 12: Wireshark and network analysis
Hafta 13: Metasploit and exploitation basics
Hafta 14: Nessus and vulnerability scanning

courses.name: Genetik Algoritma ve Programlama
courses.purpose: Bu ders, doğal seçilimden ilham alan evrimsel hesaplama tekniklerini tanıtır ve öğrencilerin bu yöntemlerin optimizasyon ile makine öğrenmesi problemlerinde nasıl kullanılacağını anlamasını sağlar.
courses.description: Bu ders, genetik algoritmalar, genetik programlama, seçim stratejileri, çaprazlama, mutasyon ve uygunluk değerlendirmesi gibi evrimsel hesaplama konularını incelemektedir. Ayrıca öğrencilerin bu teknikleri gerçek dünya problemlerine uygulaması hedeflenmektedir.

courses.name: Design Patterns
courses.purpose: This course introduces design patterns as reusable solutions to common software design problems. It aims to help students improve the maintainability, scalability, and flexibility of software systems by applying proven object-oriented design practices.
courses.description: The course covers design patterns, including creational, structural, and behavioral patterns such as Singleton, Factory, Observer, and Strategy. It emphasizes object-oriented design principles and best practices, and shows how these patterns can be applied in software projects.
learning_outcomes: Understand the role of design patterns in software engineering.
Identify and explain common creational, structural, and behavioral patterns.
Apply design patterns to improve software maintainability, scalability, and flexibility.

courses.name: Distance Education Technologies
courses.purpose: This course examines the technologies and pedagogical strategies behind online and distance learning. It aims to help students understand how digital learning environments are designed, evaluated, and used effectively for teaching and learning.
courses.description: This course covers technologies and pedagogical strategies for online and distance learning. Topics include learning management systems (LMS), video conferencing tools, content creation, and assessment methods. Students evaluate platforms such as Moodle, Canvas, and Zoom and design their own e-learning modules. The course also addresses accessibility, interaction, and instructional design.

courses.name: Fuzzy Logic
courses.purpose: This course introduces the fundamentals of fuzzy logic and its applications in systems that deal with uncertainty and imprecise data. It aims to equip students with the ability to model, analyze, and apply fuzzy systems in decision-making and control problems.
courses.description: The course covers the basics of fuzzy sets, membership functions, fuzzy inference systems, and fuzzy control. Students apply fuzzy systems in areas such as robotics, automation, and artificial intelligence for decision-making and control processes. Simulation and design tools such as MATLAB Fuzzy Logic Toolbox are used.

courses.name: Computer Graphics
courses.purpose: This course introduces the fundamental principles of computer graphics and aims to help students understand how visual content is generated, manipulated, and rendered on a computer. It also develops practical skills for building interactive graphical applications using modern graphics APIs.
courses.description: This course covers the basic principles of computer graphics, with a focus on rendering, modeling, and animation. Topics include 2D/3D transformations, lighting, shading, texture mapping, and real-time graphics. Students develop interactive visual applications using graphics APIs such as OpenGL or WebGL. Projects include creating visual simulations, games, or visualization tools.

courses.name: Database Applications
courses.purpose: This course aims to equip students with the knowledge and skills needed to design and develop database-oriented applications.
courses.description: The course covers advanced SQL, stored procedures, triggers, and database connectivity using programming languages such as Java, Python, or PHP. It also addresses transaction management, concurrency control, and database security. Students develop full-stack applications using relational or NoSQL databases.

courses.name: Deep Learning
courses.purpose: This advanced course aims to provide students with a comprehensive understanding of deep learning architectures and techniques, and to prepare them to design and evaluate modern neural network-based solutions for real-world problems.
courses.description: This course covers deep learning architectures and techniques in depth. Topics include convolutional neural networks (CNNs), recurrent neural networks (RNNs), autoencoders, generative adversarial networks (GANs), and transfer learning. Students develop models for image recognition, natural language processing, and generative tasks using frameworks such as TensorFlow and PyTorch. The course includes hands-on projects and research-oriented assignments.
